summary: Martin Fowler shares his six favorite musical discoveries of 2020, reflecting on his longtime habit of buying four albums per month and his recent shift to purchasing through Bandcamp. He highlights artists spanning jazz, world, and roots music, including GoGo Penguin, You Are Wolf, and Somi. Fowler emphasizes Bandcamp's appeal for both supporting artists directly and sampling music before purchase. The post serves as a personal music recommendation list while acknowledging the sources that led him to these discoveries.
key_points: Fowler maintains a decades-old habit of buying four albums monthly and rotating through his accumulated collection
He switched from emusic to Bandcamp because it passes more money to artists and allows pre-purchase listening
His musical tastes center on jazz influenced by minimalist classical and EDM, plus world and roots music
The six highlighted albums span Manchester jazz trio GoGo Penguin, avant-garde English folk from You Are Wolf, and international jazz collaborations
He credits Dave Sumner's Bandcamp Daily columns and the OK Jazz podcast as his best discovery sources
money_quotes: Writing about music is like dancing about architecture.
I'm still stuck in the mold of buying albums.
From what I can tell, the site passes on a bigger share of my dollars to the artist than buying through someone like Amazon.
My tastes are not mainstream, and I doubt I'd find any of these albums on my car radio.
You Are Wolf reimagines this so that it sounds ahead of its time in the twenty-first.
The resulting album is uplifting and joyful, and I hope their relationship mirrors it.
key_insight: Bandcamp has become the ideal platform for music discovery because it combines ethical artist compensation with the ability to sample before committing to a purchase.
